school_utilization_classification.backward_compatibility: Legacy near_capacity values are tolerated in frontend rendering and backend filters, but backend filters map near_capacity requests to approaching_capacity.

edge_case_verification.80_99: covered by unit tests as approaching_capacity

implementation: The right edge of the left map layer panel now has a full-height invisible resize zone.
Dragging left collapses immediately when the raw width reaches the minimum expanded width and pointer movement continues left.
Expanded panel content is removed immediately during collapsed preview to prevent squished text.
Dragging back right restores expanded preview only after the raw width reaches the minimum expanded width.
Resize state uses requestAnimationFrame and CSS variable updates; React state/localStorage are committed at pointerup.

arrow_collapse_behavior.hover_behavior: Hover changes opacity, color, border, and shadow only; no hover transform, scale, size, margin, or border-width changes.

left_panel_edge_resize.old_controls: The old grip-only collapse/resize control was replaced by the full edge resize zone plus arrow-only collapse control.

frontend_display_updates: School utilization filters and legend now display Approaching capacity seed.
School utilization map styling treats approaching_capacity and legacy near_capacity the same color profile.
Selected parcel school utilization copy now says preliminary approaching-capacity utilization.

validation_results.seed_reload: passed; 37 rows loaded with approaching_capacity distribution
validation_results.npm_dev_cfs: passed after restarting local FastAPI and Next.js services

smoke_test_results.browser_console: No captured console errors after loading localhost:3000
smoke_test_results.arrow_control: Browser check found 32x32 arrow control, correct collapse/expand labels, and no console errors after toggling
smoke_test_results.collapsed_rail: Scoped browser DOM check confirmed collapsed rail text is Layers

remaining_known_issues: Presentation-derived school utilization remains preliminary and needs official verification.
Official school-level enrollment, functional capacity, and available-seat data are still required before capacity scoring.
The full edge drag behavior was implemented and code/browser-presence verified, but tactile drag feel should still be visually reviewed by the user.
